own_review.issue: Re-enabling IS_NOT_DISTINCT_FROM in gatherConstraints lets constant inference replace BOTH sides of an equi-join key with the same constant (RelMdPredicates.eqConstant line 230 emits INDF pull-up predicates for nullable columns), eliminating the only equi key. testInnerJoinWithJoinConditionPushDown now throws TableException ('Cannot generate a valid execution plan') under BroadcastHashJoin/ShuffledHashJoin/SortMergeJoin and degrades to NestedLoopJoin with where=[true] otherwise (NestedLoopJoinTest.xml). Previously-planning queries now fail or fall back to cross-product NLJ.

Streaming consequences silently accepted in golden plans: join inputSpec degrades from JoinKeyContainsUniqueKey to NoUniqueKey (far more expensive streaming join state), and one plan becomes where=[true] with Exchange(distribution=[single]) — full cross join forced onto single parallelism.

Stale fork-rationale javadoc: class header still says the file was copied 'because of current Calcite way of inferring constants from IS NOT DISTINCT FROM clashes with filter push down' — this PR re-enables exactly that behavior, so the stated reason for the fork is now contradictory. Should be rewritten to reflect the remaining reasons (CALCITE-6764 backport, RexFieldAccess modification) vs Calcite 1.37.0 (flink-table/pom.xml:82).
Unused import: removing all 7 TABLE_EXEC_DISABLED_OPERATORS usages leaves 'import org.apache.flink.table.api.config.ExecutionConfigOptions' unused; will trip the unused-import check.

Removing the HashJoin/NestedLoopJoin disabling (added in FLINK-12168/2019 to force SortMergeJoin coverage) means these decimal join tests no longer exercise the sort-merge path; the removal is required because constant inference can eliminate the equi key for the single-row values source, making SMJ unplannable — a test-coverage workaround for the regression, doubling as e2e proof of the NLJ fallback.

NULL-literal edge case: with INDF treated as EQUALS, 'x IS NOT DISTINCT FROM NULL' now registers x -> NULL in the constant map; gatherConstraint's isConstant/conflict/CAST handling was written for EQUALS semantics where a NULL rhs is unreachable. Worth a targeted test.

NOT an issue (verification note): deleting the testInnerJoinWithJoinConditionPushDown golden XML is required and consistent — the Scala overrides now expect TableException, so no golden plan can exist; this matches the established pattern already used by neighboring overrides in those files.
Nit: updated comment 'Line 2489~2494' is approximately right — markers land at 2489~2493 after the -5 line shift (base markers 2494~2498).

points.point: RexUtil javadoc fork rationale not updated; re-enabling contradicts stated copy reason
testInnerJoinWithJoinConditionPushDown now throws TableException — previously-working query failing is a regression, not a fix; demands root-cause investigation

Golden XML removal while Scala expects exception is 'inconsistent' — FALSE: removal is required (no plan exists when planning throws) and matches the established override-and-assert pattern in those same files
NULL-literal inference edge case: 'x IS NOT DISTINCT FROM NULL' infers x=NULL; asks for targeted test of interaction with filter push-down
Line-number comment 'Line 2489~2494' fragile to drift; suggests anchors instead

missed: Streaming JoinTest.xml consequences: JoinKeyContainsUniqueKey -> NoUniqueKey and where=[true] with Exchange(single) — accepted perf regression in streaming plans
Unused import ExecutionConfigOptions in DecimalITCase.scala after removing all usages
exceeds: NULL-literal INDF inference edge case (beyond human reference)

points.point: Claims CALCITE-6764 block is 'actually around line 2497-2502' and PR's 2489~2494 is stale — FALSE: base markers are 2494-2498 and post-change 2489-2493, so the PR comment is essentially correct and v6's correction is fabricated
INDF treated identically to EQUALS in gatherConstraint, 'may have implications elsewhere' — hedged, no actionable issue surfaced

Central regression with verified root cause: RelMdPredicates.eqConstant (line 230 — exact) emits INDF predicates for nullable columns, so plain-EQUALS queries hit the new inference and join keys vanish; flags tests now expected to fail

DecimalITCase config removal is a 'positive outcome... tests previously required disabling HashJoin/NestedLoopJoin because of this exact issue... regression now fixed' — FALSE: disabling dates to FLINK-12168 (2019) to force SortMergeJoin coverage, not this issue; removal is an accommodation (SMJ becomes unplannable), and '===' is plain equality in the Table API, not null-safe
Suggest @Disabled instead of XML deletion + override — style opinion contrary to the established pattern already used in those files; correctly does not claim inconsistency
Class javadoc fork rationale now stale; suggests updating to reflect current trade-off

missed: Streaming JoinTest.xml consequences: NoUniqueKey inputSpec and Exchange(single)/where=[true] perf regression
NULL-literal INDF inference edge case (point 2 gestures at semantics but surfaces no concern)
exceeds: Exact eqConstant/INDF mechanism (sharper than the human's explanation)

qualitative: Both candidates caught the central verified regression (constant inference eliminating equi-join keys so testInnerJoinWithJoinConditionPushDown now throws TableException), and both flagged the stale fork-rationale javadoc. v5 edges ahead on net: its NULL-literal INDF edge case is a real extra finding, and its only error is the recurring false 'golden-XML removal is inconsistent' claim. v6 shows the single most impressive verification in the set — pinpointing RelMdPredicates.eqConstant line 230 as the exact INDF source, which matches the repo — but squanders it with two fabrications: a false line-number 'correction' (2497-2502 vs the actual 2494-2498 base / 2489-2493 post-change, meaning the PR comment it disputes is correct) and an inverted reading of the DecimalITCase change as 'regression now fixed' when git history shows the operator-disabling dates to FLINK-12168 (2019) and the removal merely accommodates the new planning failure. Neither candidate noticed the streaming NoUniqueKey/Exchange(single) plan degradations or the now-unused ExecutionConfigOptions import. Neither produced absence-fabrications in the strict sense.
